prompt: We're running a hands-on technical design session to work out the instant-payments processing flow and how it integrates with our systems. Who should be in the room?

assertions.text: States the meeting type / altitude it sized the room for (design / working / maker level)
assertions.rationale: New requirement: meeting type drives altitude.

assertions.text: Surfaces Ahmed Benali (Integration Architect) and Bram Hendriks (Domain Architect, Payments) as hands-on design participants
assertions.rationale: The makers/pattern-owners for an instant-payments integration design.

assertions.text: Aisha Khan (Tribe Lead, Payments) is included at a working-relevant score, NOT dropped for being too junior
assertions.rationale: A design session wants the delivery/maker level; this is the inverse of the strategy case.

assertions.text: Does not elevate a person to the top purely on seniority; a 'Head of' is only high if their discipline is core to this design
assertions.rationale: Altitude: leaders are usually too senior for a working session.

assertions.text: Flags that the actual hands-on builders (unprofiled reports such as Maria Oliveira / Jonas Bakker) are missing from the wiki
assertions.rationale: A design session needs makers; the makers aren't profiled here.

assertions.text: Every ranked stakeholder has a 1-10 score and a rationale, ordered highest first
No fabricated people in the ranked list (unprofiled wiki-link people only in the gap note)
